title: Post on Reddit unveils Lehigh University student's fraud
body: The university did not lose a thing, so it was easy for them to forgive the debt.  It was the donors to the scholarship and the students who bore the weight of this man's scam.  He will return to his home country and run remote scams from there.  The university has a duty to pursue justice against this criminal, so that he can never step onto US soil again.  Instead, they are stepping away, not in a spirit of charity, but to avoid any accountability for their own lack of diligence.  Financial benefactors to the school should take note.

title: Reading from Rate My Professors
body: Interesting concept for a video! I certainly empathize with these professors. When I taught calculus at the University of Waterloo, one student's review claimed that I "threw chairs." Since all the chairs in the lecture hall were bolted to the floor, that would have been a superhuman feat. :)

title: Reading from Rate My Professors
body:
Plato was once nearly drowned in water a waterboarding-like act fashion by his teacher.  The teacher asked him, "Do you want air to breath?"  "How bad do you want to breathe?"  "Are you ready to breathe??"  With seconds before drowning to his death, Plato arose from the water to breathe air as if it was truly his last, and Plato realized who much he wanted if not NEEDED that air.  His teacher was Socrates.  And Socrates said, "When you seek my teachings, it MUST be as undisputedly desired so much, just as you wanted that breath of air to continue living.

"The teacher will only teacher when the student is ready"
author: @1SmokingLizard

title: Reading from Rate My Professors
body: Rate my Professor is such a trash website... ALL of my Lehigh Professors were and are Amazing.  Thank you for the time and effort you put into my education.
author: @frgsinwntr

title: Reading from Rate My Professors
body: But aren't professors students, too? This makes it seem like professors don't care about what students think of their class. Do they not read class evaluations? Sure, some of these comments were outrageous, but I'm sure some of their points were valid. Seems like a lot of universities these days are hiring "professors" with no teaching experience and hand them 350 students to "teach." If you're talking to the group of students for an hour with no interaction, you're NOT teaching. You're just talking.

title: Lehigh University Reviews - Why go to Lehigh?
body: This is mostly still true today. I graduated Lehigh in 2023 when the market crashed. I also got my current job from connecting with someone in a party after I graduated. The only part that has now changed is you will definitely be getting a job within your major and actually maybe even a step down from your major because of how horrible the job market is now
author: @TheHardyboy20six

title: Lehigh University Reviews - Why go to Lehigh?
body: I have to disagree. Even if you don't get a job that relates to your major, a college degree is a must have for many opportunities
author: @lukec1750

title: Lehigh University Reviews - Why go to Lehigh?
body:
1.) The title is misleading. The video content has nothing to do with Lehigh.
2.) This talking point is getting very tired and has been totally disproven. Please just look at the statistics. Individuals without a college degree earn significantly less than individuals with a college degree (on average). Cut out this anecdotal BS about people who drop out of college and then go on to found and lead multi million dollar businesses. What you’re leaving out is that the percentage of college dropouts who end up becoming extremely successful is very, very low. For one, everyone does not have that ability, but also, a great deal of luck is involved. Given that situation, the most rational and logical course is to go to college and study something that will give you marketable skills. Just because your job has nothing to do with your major does not mean that your college degree is useless. Try getting that job without a college degree. It’s probably not going to happen.
author: @Brassard1985

title: Lehigh University Reviews - Why go to Lehigh?
body: I went into a career that had nothing to do with my undergraduate major; I changed my undergraduate major 2x. A person cant get my job without having 60 graduate credits above a bachelors, plus some other stuff.
author: @171TITO

title: Lehigh University Reviews - Why go to Lehigh?
body: My family drives a Subaru, a Honda, and a Nissan we got from my Nana after she passed. Are you gonna shit talk my parents who are middle class and work their asses off, and my Nana who did the same? Your supremacy and privilege are highly offensive here. I thought your video would be useful, though it turned out to be offensive and very fatuous, to say the least. College is not essential for everyone and it is THEIR individual choice whether they go or not. The next time you create one of these uploads, consider who is watching and be mindful of whether feelings will be hurt. You are very ineffective and inaccurate in your words. What data and statistics are you basing this off of? I'll wait...none.

title: COLLEGE MOVE IN VLOG 2021 | Moving Into My New Place | Lehigh University
body:
My university is the same. They prefer the term first year over freshman due to all the negative connotations that the term freshman has gained over the years. In order to have first years not feel so scared by phrases like “freshman Friday (which I don’t think is really a college thing)”, “freshman 15” or the common upper class-man complaining about these “damn over-eager freshman” they try to refrain from the word.

Words from a past 3 year Resident Assistant lol just my experience at my school
author: @angiegomez8123
